ARTICLE XXVIII
DRUG AND ALCOHOL ABUSE POLICY

29.01 Purpose - The purpose of this policy is to provide all members of
the bargaining unit with notice of the provisions of the Newton Police
Department's drug and alcohol testing program. It is the policy of the Newton
Police Department that a drug and alcohol free police forte must be maintained
at all times and that this requirement justifies the use of a reasonable
employee drug and alcohol testing program. The use of non-prescribed
controlled substances as defined in Chapter 94C of the Massachusetts General
Laws and other forms of drug and alcohol abuse seriously impair an officer's
physical and mental health, and thus, his/her job performance. Therefore, in
order to ensure the integrity of the Newton Police Department's officers and to
preserve public trust and confidence in a fit and drug and alcohol free police

force, there shall be a testing program to detect inappropriate drug and alcohol

50

use and, under appropriate circumstances, to provide for the rehabilitation of

any such officer.
29.02 Authorized Use of Prescribed Medications - Any officer who is

undergoing medical treatment with any prescribed drug(s) may, at his/her
option, report this information to his/her supervisor, and a determination will
be made as to that officer's ability to perform his/her regular duties while

taking such medication.
29.03 Prohibited Conduct - The following conduct by any officer is

prohibited:

A. Unauthorized use, possession, manufacture, distribution,
dispensation or sale of a non-prescribed controlled substance,
illegally used drug, drug paraphernalia, or alcohol on Department
property, on Department business, in Department supplied
vehicles, in vehicles being used for Department purposes, or
during working hours; neither this section nor Section B is
intended to be applicable to unopened containers of alcohol in an
officer's private vehicle which has been purchased for the officer's
off duty consumption;

B. Unauthorized storage in a desk, locker, automobile or other
repository on department property of any illegally-used drug, non-
prescribed controlled substance, drug paraphernalia, or alcohol
subject to the aforesaid exception in Section A;

C. Being under the influence of a non-prescribed controlled
substance, of an illegally used drug, or alcohol on Department
property, on department business, in Department supplied
vehicles or vehicle being used for Department business or during
working hours;

D. Possession, use, manufacture, distribution or sale of illegally-used
drugs or non-prescribed controlled substances while off duty;

E. Switching or adulterating any breath, blood, urine, hair or other

test sample;

51

F. Refusing consent to testing or refusing to submit a breath, urine,
blood, hair or other test sample for testing where and when
expressly required under this policy;

G. Failing to adhere to the terms of any rehabilitation agreement
which the officer has signed provided that he/she has been given
an opportunity to consult with legal counsel and/or a Union
representative before signing same;

Hi. Refusing to sign a rehabilitation agreement provided he/she has
been given an opportunity to consult with legal counsel and/or a

Union representative.

29.04A Testing Procedure
A.1. The Chief of Police, or his designee in the Chiefs absence, may

require that an officer submit to a drug and/or alcohol screening test forthwith
to detect the presence of non-prescribed drugs, illegally-used drugs, alcohol or
non-prescribed controlled substances for the reasons listed in paragraphs
29.04, B.1 to B.5. The officer being tested may, at his/her option, be
accompanied by a Union representative while the screening test is being
administered, provided that the taking of the test is not delayed thereby.

A.2. The officer may initiate a review of the Chief's directive in the case
of a drug screening test. Failure of the officer to initiate the review immediately
shall be deemed a waiver of this right. When the review procedure has been
initiated, the Chief's directive shall be reviewed by a committee of three,
comprised of either the Chief's Administrative Assistant or the Internal Affairs
Officer appointed by the Chief, one officer appointed by the Union, and the’
CITY's Director of Personnel. The review shall be conducted and concluded
within twenty-four (24) hours of the time the Chief required the test sample, or
as soon thereafter as is practicable.

A.3. Ifthe Review Committee concludes that the drug screening test is
warranted, such testing shall be conducted immediately. If the Review

Committee finds that the test is not warranted, the sample shall not be tested

52

and shall be destroyed. The decision of the Review Committee shall be final
and binding upon the parties and not subject to the grievance and arbitration
provisions of this Agreement. ,

A.4. Only a laboratory certified by the United States Department of
Health and Human Services under its Mandatory Guidelines for federal
workplace drug testing programs will administer all drug and alcohol tests.
The drug screening test will be an immunoassay screen (i.e., "EMIT") with all
positives tested for confirmation using Gas Chromatography / Mass
Spectrometry (GC/MS) technology.

A.5. There will be a chain of custody process that directly follows a
specimen from initial collection through final testing to ensure that the
specimen's security, proper identification, and integrity are not compromised.

A.6. Employees will not suffer any loss of pay or benefits during the
testing process.

A.7. . An original non-tested sample will be given to the officer upon
~ request made at the time the sample is provided.

A.8. Positive Test Results - The presence of greater than 0.04 alcohol

content in the blood, or a verified positive drug test for illegally-used drugs,
non-prescribed drugs or non-prescribed controlled substances shall be
considered a violation of this policy. An "illegally-used drug" is defined as the
ingestion of prescribed or over-the-counter medication in amounts beyond the
prescribed or recommended dosage, or taken after the prescribed period of
time.

A.9. All drug tests will be reviewed and interpreted by a qualified
physician (Medical Review Officer” or “MRO”). The MRO will review and
interpret all positive results before they are reported to the Chief. In carrying
out this responsibility, the MRO shall examine alternate medical explanations
for any positive test result. This action may include conducting a review of the
officer's medical history, the officer's medical records, and any other relevant

biomedical factors. The MRO will make reasonable attempts to contact the

53:

officer and to conduct an interview to determine if there is an acceptable,

alternative medical explanation for the positive result.

A.10. The screening test of the officer shall be administered by the City
Physician, the Nurse Practitioner, or the City Physician's designee in
accordance with this policy for drug and alcohol testing and provided that the
person administering the test has been properly certified, trained or is
otherwise qualified to administer that particular test.

A.11. The results of the drug screening test shall be given to the Chief of

‘Police and to the officer..

B. Reasons for Testing

Officers will be required to take a drug/alcohol test as a condition of

continued employment in order to ascertain prohibited drug/alcohol use, only
as provided below: .

B.1. Ifthere is a reasonable suspicion of a supervisor that an officer is
or has been using drugs or alcohol in violation of this policy. "Reasonable
suspicion" is something more than a hunch but less than probable cause. It
means a reasonable individualized suspicion, which is articulable, that the
officer violated this policy. It must be based upon specific, objective facts and
any rationally derived inferences from those facts about the conduct of an
individual that would lead a reasonable person to suspect that the officer is or
has been using drugs and/or alcohol in violation of this policy.

Examples of "reasonable suspicion" may include, but are not limited to,
the following:

(a) observable phenomena, such as direct observation of on-duty
alcohol-use or possession and/or direct observation of on-duty use
or possession of any non-prescribed drugs or illegally used drugs,
and/or the on-duty display of behaviors which appear to be
indicative of the use of any such drugs or alcohol and are not

attributable to other factors;

54

(b) a pattern of abnormal conduct or erratic behavior while on duty
(slurred speech, uncoordinated movement, gait stupor, excessive
giddiness, unexplained periods of exhilaration and excitement,
impaired judgment, etc. or deteriorating work performance,
including but not limited to, frequent absenteeism, excessive
tardiness, or frequent accidents not attributable to other factors;

(c) newly discovered evidence that the officer has tampered with a
prior drug/alcohol test;

(d) repeated or flagrant violations of the Department's rules and
procedures which are determined by a supervisor to pose a
substantial risk of injury or property damage and which are not
attributable to other factors and appear to be related to drug
and/or alcohol abuse. .

The above examples are not all inclusive, but are intended to be

illustrative.

B.2. When an officer is offered any promotional position, i.e., Sergeant,
Lieutenant, and/or Captain, asa pre-promotional condition.

B.3. When an officer is offered certain specialist assignments, as a pre-
assignment condition. The specialist assignments subject to pre-appointment
drug screening are: Administrative Aide(s) to the Chief; Community Service
Officer(s); Detective(s); Internal Affairs Officer(s); Traffic Officer(s); Youth
Officer(s).

B.4. Subsequent to any significant vehicular accident involving a
vehicle which is being operated by an officer while on duty.

B.5. Subsequent to any serious, unsafe practice or incident (such an
incident includes the unplanned, unexpected and unintended discharge of a

firearm) which occurs while the officer is on duty.

29.05A Consequences of a Positive Test

A.1. A positive test in violation of this policy or any other violation of
this policy will result in disciplinary action in accordance with the |

Department's disciplinary procedures subject to the conditions set forth below.

55

A.2. Discipline for any violation of this policy means any permitted

disciplinary action up to and including termination of employment.

A.3. Discipline for any officer who tests positive for alcohol shall, where

there is no independent violation of Department rules other than being under

the influence of alcohol, be in accordance with the following schedule:

(a)

(b)

For the first such offense - a Rehabilitation Agreement shall
be developed and signed and the officer shall be suspended
for up to a maximum of five (5) days.

For the second such offense - a further Rehabilitation
agreement shall be developed and signed and the officer
shall be suspended for up to a maximum of thirty (30) days.
For the third such offense - the officer shall be disciplined up
to and including termination of employment.

If, following the first offense of testing positive for the
presence of greater than .04 alcohol content in his/her
blood, there:is no further such offense by the officer for a
period of three (3) years from the date of the first such
offense, the first offense shall be removed from his/her
record and any subsequent such offense thereafter will be
deemed to be a first such offense for the purpose of this
progressive disciplinary procedure. However, if a second
such offense should occur within three (3) years of the first
such offense, then both offenses shall remain in the officer's
record permanently.

After the completion of any Rehabilitation Program that is a
part of any Rehabilitation Agreement provided for hereunder,
an officer shall be subject to unannounced follow-up testing
for three (3) years following his/her return to full duties.
There shall be a maximum of six (6) such unannounced
follow-up tests during any twelve (12) month period;

however, this limitation shall not be applicable to any

56


alcohol test that is administered for reasonable cause

pursuant to this policy. If any such follow-up test yields a

positive result, the officer shall be immediately subject to

further disciplinary action.in accordance with this policy.
A.4. Officers shall retain their Civil Service rights, as provided in G.L. c.

31, and their contractual rights under Article XXXI, Just Cause in connection
with any disciplinary action that may be taken against them pursuant to this
policy. However, as well, all other aspects of this policy are subject to the
contractual grievance and arbitration procedure except as provided in Section

28.04 A.3.
A.5. Positive Drug Screen- An employee testing positive for drugs will be

given one opportunity for rehabilitation and shall continue to receive health
insurance benefits during the rehabilitation period under the same terms and
conditions as all other members of the bargaining unit. An officer may be
terminated upon the occurrence of a second positive drug test.

29.06 Assistance Programs - The Department shall, in addition to taking

any disciplinary actions, refer any officer who is found in violation of this policy
to the Employee Assistance Program for assessment, counseling, and referral
for treatment or rehabilitation, as appropriate.

29.07 Voluntary Assistance - Disciplinary action will not be imposed for
an employee who volunteers for drug or alcohol testing prior to being identified
through other means or who obtains counseling or rehabilitation voluntarily
through the Employee Assistance Program and thereafter refrains from using

illegal drugs or alcohol.

ARTICLE XXXI
JUST CAUSE

No employee, who has completed his/her probationary period, shall be
disciplined or discharged without just cause. The filing of a grievance by any
employee contesting any discipline or discharge shall constitute an election of
remedies which will automatically bar the filing of an appeal by such employee
to the Civil Service Commission under Chapter 31, or to the Retirement Board

under Chapter 71, dealing with the same matter.

ARTICLE XXXII
TIME OFF FOR UNION BUSINESS

business to on-duty officers of the UNION and members of the UNION's Executive
Board: |

1. Collective bargaining meetings with the City, including mediation

sessions and/or arbitration sessions scheduled by the Joint Labor-

Management Committee for Municipal Police and fire: 4 officers.

ee Grievance hearings, including mediation and/or arbitration hearings
scheduled through the American Arbitration Association: 3 officers;

disciplinary matters: 3 officers;

4. Hearings before the Massachusetts Civil Service Commission: 3
officers;

3. Hearings before the Massachusetts Labor Relations Commission: 3
officers;

6. Meetings in preparation for item numbers 1 through 5 above and

bimonthly meetings of the UNION's Executive Board: not more than
4 officers;

the Chief of Police, or his designee (letter of explanation to be
submitted by UNION); |
8. Effective upon ratification, up to four (4) officials shall be granted
time off without loss of pay or benefits to attend semi-annual
meetings of the Association; and
9. The Association’s secretary shall be granted time off without loss of

ARTICLE XXXII
TIME OFF FOR EMPLOYMENT-RELATED HEARINGS

34.01 For so-called “appointing authority hearings” held under the
provisions of M.G.L. c. 31, the civil service law, the individual who is the subject
of the hearing shall be released from duty to attend the hearing in the event it is

scheduled to occur during a tour of duty.

~ 60

34.02 For cases brought to arbitration by the UNION, the named
grievant{s], if any shall be released from duty to attend the hearing in the event it
is scheduled to occur during his/her/their tour of duty. In the event of a
“sroup”, or “class action” grievance, the UNION, through its counsel, shall
undertake in good faith to determine which bargaining unit employees it wishes
to attend the hearing, and shall communicate that request to the CITY. In the
event the CITY maintains that the simultaneous release of.all of the identified
bargaining unit members would impose an unreasonable burden on the
Department, the parties shall in good faith discuss alternate arrangements to
_ accommodate their respective interests. |

34.03 For proceedings before the Civil Service Commission, the named
appellant[s] shall be released from duty to attend the hearing in the event it is
scheduled to occur during his/her/their tour of duty.

34.04 For hearings before the Labor Relations Commission in which the
UNION is the sole named charging party, the UNION through its counsel, shall
undertake in good faith to determine which bargaining unit employees it wishes
to attend the hearing, and shall communicate that request to the CITY. In the
event the UNION requests the attendance of multiple bargaining unit members,
and the CITY maintains that such release would impose an unreasonable burden
on the Department, the parties shall in good faith discuss alternate
arrangements to accommodate their respective interests.

34.05 For hearings before the Labor Relations Commission in which one
of more bargaining unit members are also named as charging parties along with.
the UNION, those named unit members shall be released from duty to attend the
hearing ! in the event it is scheduled to occur during his/her/their tour of duty.

34.06 In any of the proceedings just identified, should the UNION or the -
bargaining unit member/party in interest wish to call as witnesses any members:
of the bargaining unit, the UNION shall inform the CITY in advance of the
hearing as to their identity and they shall be released, with the understanding
that they shall return to duty upon the completion of their testimony if sufficient

time remains until the scheduled end of the tour. When possible, such witnesses

61

shall be “on call” for hearings held in the City of Newton and summoned to the

hearing from their duty at the time that their testimony is required.

ARTICLE XXXIV
FIELD TRAINING

35.01 The Department will maintain a Field Training Officer Program
(Program) to provide orientation and training to all newly hired patrol officers.
The Program shall extend over a period of ten (10) consecutive weeks, the first
week of which shall be classroom orientation and the last nine weeks of which
shall consist of field training. .

35.02 The Department shall appoint patrol officers to serve on a voluntary
basis as Field Training Officers (FTO’s). All Patrol officers so designated shall
receive training for that role over a three (3) day training course and shall be
certified by the Department as FTO’s upon the completion of such training.
During this training period, officers normally assigned to last half shifts or first
half shifts shall be reassigned to the day shift without loss of pay or benefits.
Officers who attend training on their day(s) off shall be paid for all such training
shifts at the rate of time and one-half their regular rate of pay or shall receive
compensatory time at the rate of time and one-half, at the officer’s option.

35.03 FTO’s shall provide training to newly hired officers on each of their
shifts over the last nine-week period of the Program. For each shift on which
officers serve as FTO’s, they shall each receive an additional hour of pay beyond
the number of hours actually worked, at the rate of time and one-half their
regular rate of pay or shall receive compensatory time at the rate of time and one-

half in lieu of such pay, at the officer’s option.

ARTICLE XXXV
RESIDENCY ALLOWANCE

Effective July 1, 2016, within nine (9) months of employment by the City, a
police officer must reside within the City of Newton or at any other place in the

Commonwealth that is within thirty-five (35) miles of the perimeter of the City of

62

Newton, except that all officers within the Association’s bargaining unit employed

by the City prior to July 1, 2016 shall be exempt from this provision.

APPENDIX C

A “Serious Health Condition” means an illness, injury impairment, or physical
or mental condition that involves one of the following:

1. Hospital Care

Inpatient care (i.e., an overnight stay) in a hospital, hospice, or residential
medical care facility, including any period of incapacity? or subsequent
treatment in connection with or consequent to such inpatient care.

2. Absence Plus Treatment

(a) A period of incapacity? of more than three consecutive calendar days
(including any subsequent treatment or period of incapacity? relating to the
same condition), that also involves:

(1) Treatment’ two or more times by a health care provider, by a nurse
or physician’s assistant under direct supervision of a health care
provider, or by a provider of health care services (e.g. physical therapist)
under orders of, or on referral by, a health care provider; or

(2) Treatment by a health care provider on at least one occasion which
results in a regimen of continuing treatment* under the supervision of
the health care provider.

3. Pregnancy

Any period of incapacity due to pregnancy, or for prenatal care.

4. Chronic Conditions Requiring Treatments
A chronic condition which:

(1) Requires periodic visits for treatment by a health care provider, or by
a nurse or physician’s assistant under direct supervision of a health care
provider;

(2) Continues over an extended period of time (including recurring
episodes of a single underlying condition); and

(3) May cause episodic rather than a continuing period of incapacity?
(e.g., asthma, diabetes, epilepsy, etc).
68 ©


5. Permanent/Long-term Conditions Requiring Supervision

A period of incapacity? whichis permanent or long-term due to a condition for
which treatment may not be effective. The employee or family member must be
under the continuing supervision of, but need not be receiving active treatment
by, a health care provider. Examples include Alzheimer’s, a severe stroke, or
the terminal stages of a disease.

6. Multiple Treatments (Non-Chronic Conditions)

Any period of absence to receive multiple treatments (including any period of
recovery therefrom) by a health care provider or by a provider of health care
services under orders of, or on referral by, a health care provider, either for
restorative surgery after an accident or other injury, or for a condition that

. would likely result in a period of incapacity? of more than three consecutive
calendar days in the absence of medical intervention or treatment, such as
cancer (chemotherapy, radiation, etc.), severe arthritis (physical therapy), and
kidney disease (dialysis).

This optional form may be used by employees to satisfy a mandatory
requirement to furnish a medical certification (when requested) from a health
care provider, including second or third opinions and recertification (29 CFR

825.306).

Note: Persons are not required to respond to this collection of information
unless it displays a currently valid OMB control number.

3. Treatment includes examinations to determine if a serious condition exists
and evaluations of the condition. Treatment does not include routine physical
examinations, eye examinations, or dental examinations.

4+ A regimen of continuing treatment includes, for example, a course of
prescription medicine (e.g. an antibiotic) or therapy requiring special
equipment to resolve or alleviate the health condition. A regimen of treatment
does not include the taking of over-the-counter medications such as aspirin,
antihistamines, or salves; or bed rest, drinking fluids, exercise, and other
similar activities that can be initiated without a visit to a health care provider.

                                        GRIEVANCE PROCEDURE

Section 1.      A Grievance is defined as a dispute, which may arise over the application, meaning or

                Step 1.           The Union steward shall submit the grievance in to the department head
                                  within seven (7) calendar days after the occurrence of the incident upon
                                  which the grievance is based or seven (7) calendar days after the employee
                                  knew or should have known of the occurrence of the incident stating the
                                  specific article and section of the agreement that is being disputed and
                                  giving details of the incident. The Chief of Police shall answer the
                                  grievance within fourteen (14) calendar days, but may extend the time of
                                  his answer of the grievance upon written notice thereof to the employee
                                  stating his reasons for the extension. The length of time the department
                                  head may extend his answer to the grievance shall be no more than seven
                                  (7) additional calendar days.

                Step 2.           If the matter is still not resolved the grievance shall be filed in writing
                                  with the Board of Selectmen within seven (7) calendar days after receipt
                                  by the Union of the Police Chiefs reply or failure to reply. Within
                                                      3
                               fourteen (14) calendar days of such notice, the selectmen shall provide a
                               written disposition of the grievance. The meeting with the Board of
                               Selectmen shall not be a full hearing but an informal meeting for the
                               purpose of resolving the grievance and either side may at their option
                               elect confidentially of such meetings provided they have given written
                               notice of the same to the other party within a reasonable period of time.

             Step 3.           If the matter is still not resolved either party may within twenty-one (21)
                               calendar days after the date of the Selectmen's written disposition to the
                               employee submit the matter either to the Georgetown Personnel Director
                               or the American Arbitration Association. In either case, the decision of
                               the Personnel Director or the American Arbitration Association shall be
                               final and binding.

Section 3.   Neither the Personnel Director nor the Arbitrator shall have jurisdiction or

             Step 1 and go directly to Step 2.

Section 7.   The time limits expressed in this contract may be extended by both parties by agreement
             in writing prior to the time to be extended.

                                             ARTICLE 6
                                          HOURS OF WORK

Section 1.    The regular workweek for permanent, Sergeants and Detective Sergeant shall be
              scheduled on a four (4) days on, two (2) days off schedule. A shift is defined as eight
              (8) straight hours.

Section 2.    The rank of Lieutenant will work a 5/2 shift with the hours of work Monday thru Friday
              7:00AM to 3:00PM with weekends and holidays off and will receive five (5) additional
              compensatory days as a result of an administrative shift assignment.

Section 3.    All Shift Changes will be assigned at least thirty (30) days prior to the anticipated
              shift change, with the exception of a change in manpower. All shifts are subject to the
              approval of the Chief of Police. Any changes made by the Chief of Police shall not be
              arbitrary or capricious.

Section 4,    It is understood that the possibility of being called out for an emergency is part of the
              job, inasmuch as the safety of the public is involved; and as such the employee shal! be
              required to report to work when directed.

              of regular duty hours, he shall be paid four (4) hours at time and one half (1 1/2) for any

                whole or part of any day or at the option of the Sergeant and Detective Sergeant take
                compensatory time off.

 Section 6,     Overtime shall be paid at the rate of one and one half (1 1/2) times their regular rate of
                pay for time which is in excess of their regular work week.

 Section 7.     In the event that a Sergeant, Lieutenant, or Detective Sergeant is called in for work
                outside regular hours, he shall be paid four (4) hours at time and one half (1 1/2) his
                regular rate of pay.

Section 8.      If a vacancy exists of five (5) or less scheduled work days, because of sick leave, all
                Command Staff members shall be offered the vacancy on a rotating basis. If a vacancy
                of more than five (5) scheduled work days exists, filling of the vacancy shall be at the
                discretion of the Chief of Police. Each and every sick leave event (call-in) shall be
                considered separate from any and all previous sick leave events (call-ins) and shall be
                treated as a new event unless considered a pre-existing condition with documentation
                specific to each event.

Section 9.      Personal Leave. All employees within the bargaining unit shall be allowed three (3)
                personal days per fiscal year, not to be charged to any other leaves.

Section 10.     Superior Officers shall receive four (4) compensatory days per year for on call
                status.

Section 11.     All offered overtime shifts will be distributed to members on a rotating basis. The
                rotation shall be based on the number of shifts offered starting with the most senior
                person. IE: The most senior officer with the least offerings shall be offered an OT
                shift first and so-on until the full-time list is exhausted. Training for specific
                officers shall not be included in the rotation.

                                               ARTICLE 7
                                         TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT

New employees shall be on probation for six (6) months. The Town retains the right to dismiss a
probationary employee without recourse to the grievance or arbitration procedure, and he/she shall have
no seniority status nor bidding nor bumping rights.

                                                ARTICLE 8
                                       SUSPENSION AND DISCHARGE

No employee shall be suspended or discharged (other than temporary) without sufficient cause and the
employer shall notify the union in writing forty-eight (48) hours after any suspension or discharge of an
employee.
                                                ARTICLE 9
                                                 SENIORITY

The principle of seniority shall govern and control in all cases of decrease of working force as weil as
priority in extra work and selection of vacation. Seniority shall begin on the date of hire to a permanent
vacancy. In the event that more than one Officer is appointed to full-time status on the same date/time,
seniority will be based on reserve time appointments.

                                              HOLIDAYS

Section 1.     The following days shall be recognized as paid, legal holidays:

              New Year's Day                              Labor Day
              Martin Luther King Jr. Day                  Columbus Day
              President's Day                             Veteran's Day
              Patriot's Day                               Thanksgiving Day
              Memorial Day                                Day after Thanksgiving
              Independence Day                            Christmas Day

Section 2.     If the holiday occurs within and employee's vacation period, he shall be paid at his/her
               basic hourly rate in addition to his holiday pay or be entitled to a compensatory day off.

Section 3,     Employees who are scheduled to work on holidays shall be paid at one and one half
               (1 1/2) times their straight time in addition to their holiday pay or be entitled to
               compensatory time off at the discretion of the Employee. Employees working an
               overtime shift, called in to work, or mandated to work (overtime shift) will be paid at
               two (2) times their straight time rate in addition to their holiday pay or be entitled
               to compensatory time off at the discretion of the Chief of Police.

Section 4.     Except as provided for in Article 10, Section 2 and 3 there shall be no duplication of
               Holiday pay if the day is otherwise paid for.

               time off to be taken by the Lieutenant, Sergeants and Detective Sergeant.

Section 6.     In the event an employee requests time off by use of compensatory time owed, written
               notice must be submitted at least ten (10) calendar days prior to said time off.

                                              ARTICLE 11
                                              VACATIONS

Section 1.     All employees who have worked for the employer for one (1) calendar year from the date
               of employment shall receive two (2) weeks' vacation (10 days paid).

Section 2,     All employees who have worked for the employer for five (5) continuous calendar years
               from the date of employment shall receive three (3) weeks' vacation.

               One vacation day will be added for each additional year between six (6) and ten (10)
               when the employee becomes eligible for four (4) weeks' vacation. IE: An employee
               with seven (7) years' service will receive three (3) weeks + two (2) additional days.
Section 3.   All employees who have worked for the employer for ten (10) continuous calendar years
             from the date of employment shall receive four (4) weeks' vacation.

Section 4.   All employees shall be allowed one (1) additional vacation day for each year worked
             beyond their tenth 10" year up to a maximum often (10) additional days.

Section5.    Request for vacation time shall be submitted at least ten (10) calendar days in advance
             except for emergencies for approval purposes.

Section 6.   All employees under this Agreement shall be afforded the opportunity of carrying over
             one (1) week of vacation time from fiscal year to fiscal year at the discretion of the Chief
             of Police. Any employee wishing to carry over vacation time must provide notice to the
             Chief of Police no less than thirty (30) days before the end of the then current fiscal
             year.

Section 7.   Vacation periods shall run with the Town's fiscal year, July 1* to June 30".

                                            ARTICLE 12
                                            SICK LEAVE

Section 1.   Employees shall accumulate sick leave at one day and one half (1 1/4) days per month
             up to a maximum of two hundred (200) days.

             For new (7/1/2011 or after) hires, the maximum sick leave accrual shall be one-hundred
             and fifty (150) days.

Section 2.   Upon termination of employment or death (to estate), employees shall be allowed to buy
             back fifty (50%) percent, up to 75 days, of their accumulated sick leave at the rate of
             ninety ($90.00) dollars per day. However, in the event that an employee is killed in the
             line of duty working for the Town of Georgetown, the estate of the employee shall
             receive all accumulated sick time (up to 200 days) of the deceased employee at the rate
             of one hundred percent (100%) of the employee's pay.

Section 3.   At the end of the fiscal year, unit members who have not used any sick days in the
             fiscal year shall be granted three days of compensatory time. Any unit member who
             has used one sick day in the fiscal year shall be granted two days of compensatory
             time. A unit member who has used two sick days in the fiscal year shall be granted one
             day of compensatory time. No compensatory time shall be granted for use of three or
             more sick days.

Section 4.   Command Staff may give the sick bank up to ten (10) sick days per year. Sick days
             received from the sick bank shall be exempt from any and all compensation upon
             termination of employment. Sick days donated to the sick bank shall not be considered
             as used days under Article 12, Section 3.
                                             ARTICLE 13
                                         BEREAVEMENT LEAVE

Section 1.     In the event of death in the immediate family of an employee, the employee will be granted
               leave with pay in the amount of three (3) working days, and such leave will not be charged
               to sick leave or vacation leave. Bereavement leave shall cover the time absent during the
               patrolman's normal work week excluding holidays, vacation, or other time normally
               worked.

Section 2.      Immediate family shall mean spouse, children, brother, sister, mother, father, mother-in-
                law, father-in-law, foster children, stepchildren, grandchildren, significant other,
                grandparent, brother-in-law and sister-in-law. If extenuating circumstances require
                additional days for bereavement, up to three (3) additional days may be granted by the
                department head. These additional days may be taken from any and all authorized paid
                leave (except sick time), or as unpaid leave.

Section 3.     There shall be no duplication of bereavement leave if the day is otherwise paid for.

                                              ARTICLE 14
                                           MILITARY LEAVE

An official leave of absence of six (6) months or more without pay will be granted for active duty in any
branch of the Armed Forces of the United States in the event of a national emergency or war. When the
person is released from such service, he/she will be eligible for re-employment provided he notifies the
Town in writing twenty-one (21) days prior to his/her return to employment.

                                                 ARTICLE 15
                                                  JURY PAY

The employer agrees to make up the difference in any employee's wages between a normal week's wages
and compensation received for jury duty. The employee agrees to return to work if excused from jury duty
by 1:00AM on the same day.

                                             ARTICLE 16

There shall be a uniform and equipment allowance for the Police Department employees, which shall be
paid to the provider of uniforms in the amount of $1,200.00 per fiscal year and for which up to twenty-
five (25) percent can be used for cleaning per year. A terminated employee must reimburse the town for
current year’s allowance on a prorated basis. Up to $300.00 per year may be used to purchase appropriate
attire for the purpose of court appearances. Uniform allowance must be used within the fiscal year of be
forfeited.

A portion of the uniform allowance may also be used for the purchase of approved equipment. As with the
 purchase of uniforms, payment for any such equipment purchases shall be made to the equipment provider.

This agreement is entered into by the Georgetown School Department, hereinafter referred to as
the Employer, and the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees, AFL-
CIO, Mass. Public Employees Council 93, and Local 939, representing Custodians, hereinafter
referred to as the Union.



                                          PREAMBLE
INTENT AND PURPOSE:

The general intent and purpose of this Agreement is in the mutual interests of Georgetown; to
provide for the operation of our school buildings under methods which will further, to the fullest
extent possible, the safety, welfare and health of the school children of the town under conditions
which will insure economy of operation, quality and quantity of performance, cleanliness of
buildings and protection of the property.

It is further the intention and purpose of the parties hereto that this Agreement provide an orderly
collective bargaining relationship between the Employer and the Union; the establishment of an
equitable and peaceful procedure for the resolution of differences; and the establishment of rates
of pay, hours of work and other conditions of employment.

In recognition of the fact that the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts vest
responsibility to the people of Georgetown in the School Committee for the quality of education
in, and the efficient and economical operation of, the Georgetown School Department, it is
herein agreed that except as specifically and directly modified by express language in a specific
provision of this AGREEMENT, that the Committee retains all rights and powers that it has or
may hereafter be granted by law in managing the School Department and directly the work force
and may exercise the same at its discretion without any such exercise being made the subject of a
grievance except as expressly provided herein.

It is recognized that, in addition to other functions and responsibility, the Employer has and will
retain the sole right and responsibility to direct the operations of the employees and in this
connection to determine the methods, processes and type of work to be performed; the schedule
of shifts and hours of work; and to select, hire and demote employees, including the right to
make and apply rules and regulations of discipline, efficiency and safety. The Employer shall
also have the right and responsibility to discharge or otherwise discipline any employees for just
cause, to promote, and transfer, and to lay off because of lack of work or other cause unless
otherwise hereinafter provided.

                                          ARTICLE I

                                       RECOGNITION
The Employer recognized the Union as the sole and exclusive bargaining agent for the purpose
of establishing salaries, wages, hours and other conditions of employment for all custodians,
matrons and maintenance employees of the Georgetown School Department, excluding the

                                                 5
 Director of Buildings and Grounds and hereafter referred to as the supervisor and other
 supervisory employees of said school department.

The Employer will not aid, promote or finance any labor group or organization which purports to
engage in collective bargaining, or make any agreement with any such group or individual for
the purpose of undermining the Union or changing any condition contained in this
AGREEMENT.

The district reserves the right to outsource custodial/maintenance in the event of any emergency
or during a case of unforeseen circumstances that require contracting additional labor and/or
contractors with specialized skills and equipment on a temporary basis.

Such agreement will be mutually agreed upon by the school district and the union and will not
exceed a period of six (6) months unless all parties agree to an extension.

No outsourcing will be done to replace existing or routine custodial/maintenance duties.

                                         ARTICLE       II

                         UNION DUES AND INITIATION FEE

Employees may tender the initiation fee (if any) and monthly membership dues by signing the
Authorization of Dues Form. During the life of this AGREEMENT and in accordance with the
terms of the Form of Authorization of Check-Off of dues hereinafter set forth, the Employer
agrees to deduct union membership dues levied in accordance with the Constitution of the Union
from the pay of each employee who executes or has executed such form and remit the aggregate
amount to the Treasurer of the union along with a list of employees who had said dues deducted.
Such remittance shall be made monthly.

                                        ARTICLE UI

                         DISCRIMINATION AND COERCION
There shall be no discrimination by the Employer or agents of the School Department against
any employee because of his activity or membership in the Union. The Employer further agrees
that there will be no discrimination against any member for his adherence to any provision of this
AGREEMENT or his refusal to comply with any order, which would violate this
AGREEMENT.

                                        ARTICLE IV

                               GRIEVANCE PROCEDURE
Definition: for the purpose of this AGREEMENT a grievance shall be defined as a complaint by
an employee in the unit covered by this agreement, which involves an alleged violation or
misinterpretation of a specific provision of this agreement.

Since it is important that grievances be processed as rapidly as possible, the number of days
indicated at each level should be considered as maximum and every effort should be made to
 expedite the process. The time limits specified may be extended only by prior mutual
 agreement.

 Grievances shall be settled in the following manner:

 Step 1. The Union Steward, with the aggrieved employee, shall take up the grievance or dispute
 with the employee’s immediate supervisor within five (5) working days of the date of the
 grievance or his knowledge of its occurrence. The employee will provide a written statement of
 grievances citing specific language violations. The supervisor shall attempt to adjust the matter
 and shall respond to the steward within eight (8) working days.

Step 2. Ifthe grievance still remains unresolved, it shall be presented to the Superintendent of
Schools in writing within five (5) working days after the response of the Supervisor. After
having received the grievance in writing, the Superintendent shall meet with the aggrieved
employee(s) and/or a representative of the union and the steward within five (5) working days.
The Superintendent shall respond in writing within fifteen (15) working days.
The number of days indicated in the above procedure may be extended by mutual agreement due
to any absence of the parties to the procedure. Grievance involving disciplinary or discharge
action shall be processed beginning at the second step.

Step 3. Ifthe grievance is still not settled and it is related to non-personnel actions, it shall be
presented to the School Committee at its next regular meeting. Grievance hearings will be
conducted in executive sessions. The Committee shall hear the grievance and respond in writing
to the Union within fifteen (15) working days.

Step 4. Ifthe grievance is still unsettled, either party may, within twenty (20) working days after
the reply of the School Committee, by written notice to the other, request arbitration.

                                        ARTICLE IV-B

                                        ARBITRATION
A. In the even either party elects to submit a grievance to arbitration, the arbitrator shall be
selected according to and governed by the following procedure: The arbitrator is to be mutually
selected by the Committee and the Union. If the Committee and the Union cannot agree, within
seven (7) working days after written notice specified above of the intention to arbitrate then the
party demanding arbitration shall within seven (7) working days thereafter, request the American
Arbitration Association to provide a panel of arbitrators. Said arbitrator is then to be selected
under the provisions of the Voluntary Labor Arbitration Rules now, or hereafter, in effect which
shall also govern the procedure at the hearing.

B. The fees of the American Arbitration Association and of the arbitrators and the expense of
any required hearings shall be shared equally by the Committee and the Union, but each party
shall bear the expense of its representative, participants, witnesses, and for the preparation and
representation of its own case.

C. The arbitrator’s award shall be in writing and shall set forth his findings of fact with
reasoning and conclusions. He shall arrive at his decision solely upon the facts, evidence, and
contentions presented by the parties through the arbitration proceeding. The arbitrator shall have
no power to add to, subtract from, or modify any of the terms of this agreement, and in reaching
his decision shall interpret the Agreement in accordance with the principle that there are no
restrictions intended on the right or authority of the Committee other than those expressly set
forth herein. Subject to the foregoing, the decision of the arbitrator shall be submitted to the
School Committee and the Union and shall be final and binding upon the Committee, the Union
and the Custodian or group of Custodians who initiated the grievance.

D. The parties may, but mutual agreement, submit more than one pending grievance to the same
arbitrator.

                                          ARTICLE V

                                           SENIORITY
For the purpose of this article, seniority shall be considered the length of service of the
employee’s continuous service in the Georgetown Public Schools. Continuous services means
the most recent period of unbroken service in the Georgetown Public Schools, provided that
authorized leave of absence, military service, or lay off shall not be considered a break in
continuous service for the purpose of establishing seniority rating.*

In the event that a member of another bargaining unit joins the custodial/maintenance contract,
their accumulated benefits will carry over (sick leave and vacation) however, if there is a
reduction in force, seniority will be calculated based on their time in the custodial/maintenance
bargaining unit.

When determining choice of shift, the Superintendent or his/her designee will consider the
following criteria:

                     Employee qualifications


             aYeN-
                     Employee performance
                     Needs of district
                     Years of service

When all criteria are equal, the employee with the most years of service will be granted their
request.

All temporary, part-time and permanent part-time employees will be let go first, before any
permanent employees.

Qualifications being equal, the principle of seniority shall be strongly considered in cases of
promotion within the bargaining unit and lateral transfers to open positions.

*All prior agreements with current employees in exception to this shall remain in effect.
                                          ARTICLE VI

                              JOB POSTING AND BIDDING
When a position covered by this Agreement becomes vacant, such vacancy shall be posted
within seven (7) days of occurrence of vacancy in a conspicuous place listing pay, duties and
qualifications. The notice of vacancy shall remain posted for seven (7) days. Employees
interested shall apply in writing within the seven-day period. Within seven (7) days of expiration
of the posting period, the Superintendent of Schools will temporarily award the position to the
most senior applicant qualified, as determined by the Superintendent who has final approval or
disapproval of such appointment.

The successful applicant shall be given a one hundred and twenty calendar (120) day trial and
training period in the new position at the applicable rate of pay. If at the end of the trial and
training period it is determined by the Superintendent that the employee is not qualified to
perform the work, he shall be returned to his old position and rate.

If it is not mutually agreed by the parties that an applicant is qualified, the Superintendent may
fill the position outside the bargaining unit.

In addition, if the position is filled from outside the bargaining unit, all vacation benefits
contained within the contract would begin to accrue following the 120 day trial and training
period. Vacation benefits will be awarded for the trial and training period retroactive to the
initial day of employment. This paragraph is applicable to any employee hired after July 1, 2002
and who has completed their 120 day trial and training period.

                                         ARTICLE VII

                                      HOURS OF WORK
The regular hours of work each day shall be consecutive, except for interruptions for lunch
periods.

The workweek shall consist of five (5) consecutive eight (8) hour days, in a seven (7) day period
for a total of forty (40) hours per week.

Each employee shall be scheduled to work a shift with regular starting and quitting times.
Except for emergency situations; work schedules shall not be changed unless the changes are
mutually agreed upon by the Union and the Employer.

For example: If an employee is scheduled to work a 7:00 AM — 3:00 PM shift, the shift cannot
be changed to 8:00 AM — 4:00 PM without the agreement of the Union.

Between July 1* and the last week in August, employees may choose the option to work an
alternative summer schedule. The summer hours shall consist of four (4) consecutive ten (10)
hour days in a seven (7) day period for a total of forty (40) hours per week. For summer
scheduling, each employee shall work on Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday; employees shall
not be required to work on Monday or Friday at the election of the employee with the

                                                  9
understanding that the building will be covered and scheduling will be at the discretion of the
Director of Buildings and Grounds.

Summer Hours Leave: All leave benefits will be converted into hours, based on the appropriate
work week of 35-40 hours, for all employees on an approved four (4) day work week. In doing
so, the parties acknowledge that no employee is subject to any loss or realize any gain in
contractual benefits as a result of the change in calculation from days to hours, and that said
change is made merely to ensure equal and accurate benefit accrual and to facilitate
administration and accounting of benefits. If an employee wanted to take a week off for vacation
then the forty (40) hours would calculate to five (5) vacation days. If an employee just wanted to
take a single vacation day off, it would calculate to 1.2 vacation days used. The same would
apply to sick and personal time.

Summer Hours Holidays: When the holiday falls on an employee’s scheduled work day, the
employee will only be scheduled to work his/her remaining three (3) days that week. The
employee will work ten (10) hours on each of the remaining three (3) work days. When the
holiday falls on a day when the employee is already scheduled to be off, the Director of
Buildings and Grounds shall grant an alternate day off, in lieu of the holiday during the same pay
period in which the holiday falls. On that day, the employee will be compensated for ten (10)
hours of holiday pay. The employee will work ten (10) hours on each of the two remaining work
days.

                                       ARTICLE VIII

                                          OVERTIME
Employees covered by this agreement shall be paid overtime at the rate of one and one-half times
the regular rate of pay for work in excess of eight (8) hours in one day and forty (40) hours in
one week, except for Sundays’ when the rate of pay will be two (2) times the regular rate of pay
for work in excess of eight (8) hours in one day and forty (40) hours in one week.

Any employee called back to work on the same day after having completed his assigned work
and left his place of employment and before his next regular scheduled starting time, shall be
paid at the rate of time and a half for all hours worked in recall. He will be guaranteed minimum
of two (2) hours pay at time and one-half. Weekend and holiday overtime shall be a minimum of
three (3) hours.

Overtime shall be equally and impartially distributed among all personnel who ordinarily
perform such related work in the normal course of their work week.

The employer shall keep records of the overtime work, in cases of a grievance involving such
records, they shall be subject to examination of the union representative or the shop steward.

 Overtime shall be required, including Winter snow removal. If an employee will be out of town
during the Winter months a five (5) day notice must be given to the Director of Buildings and
Grounds or the employee will be held to the mandatory directive. There shall be no
discrimination against any employee who declines overtime, however recognizing not everyone


                                                10
will work the same amount of overtime, if shifts need to be filled, the employees scheduled for
the least amount of time will be expected to work.

The school Principal and the Director of Buildings and Grounds shall have the responsibility of
determining when a custodian is required for outside functions in school facilities with the
understanding that if the facility is being used after hours, either an administrator or a custodian
shall be present. The custodian in for this extra work will be paid time and one-half his regular
rate for a minimum of two (2) hours (guaranteed minimum extra call-in pay). This condition
shall be incorporated into the school use permit application so that all groups and/or
organizations will be aware that they may be required to pay for this custodial service. While
working overtime, the custodian may be assigned to work in an adjacent area. However, his
primary concern shall be in his overtime area.

In lieu of overtime pay, employees may elect to receive compensatory time, at the rate of on and
a half hours for every hour worked, not to exceed twenty-four (24) accumulated hours in a
calendar year, to be used during the calendar year. There is no carry over or monetary buy back
of unused days.



                                           ARTICLE IX

                                 UNION REPRESENTATIVES
A written list of Union representatives shall be furnished to the Employer immediately after their
designation and the Union shall notify the Employer of any changes.

                                            ARTICLE X

                                         MEAL PERIODS
Employees who for any reason work beyond their regular quitting time in to the next shift a
minimum of two (2) hours or more shall receive a meal break before they start to work on such
next shift.

                                           ARTICLE XI

                                            VACATIONS
Employees shall earn and be granted vacations as follows:

         For one (1) year of service but less than five (5) years: two (2) weeks.


  VOWS
         For five (5) years of service but less than ten (10) years: three (3) weeks.
         For ten (10) years of service or more:   four (4) weeks.
         Custodians currently with five (5) weeks of vacation shall be grandfathered with five (5)
         weeks of vacation.
  m      For 16 years of service four (4) weeks and one (1) day
         For 17 years of service four (4) weeks and two (2) days
         For 18 years of service four (4) weeks and three (3) days

                                                   11
          For 19 years of service four (4) weeks and four (4) days
          For 20 years of service five (5) weeks



Vacation time is non-cumulative. Vacation may be granted by mutual agreement of the
Employer and the employee or by a posting system. However, if a conflict should arise between
employees’ vacation schedules, the senior employee shall be given preference. Employees shall
use a minimum of 25% of their vacation days from July 1 through August 15 and use the
remaining vacation days on demand with a ten (10) day notice, except for the three (3) school
vacation weeks, when only one member of the bargaining unit will be eligible to take vacation
time subject to seniority on a rotating basis. If a member who is eligible to use the vacation time
only uses a portion of the school vacation week, the remaining portion shall be offered in
accordance with the rotation list. Granting of said vacation will be at the discretion of the
Superintendent of Schools or his/her designee.

Employees shall be allowed to carryover one (1) week vacation from year to year provided said
employee requests the carryover in writing sixty (60) days in advance.

Upon termination of employment, the employee shall receive payment equal to the amount of the
vacation pay he would have received had the termination not occurred. If the termination is
caused by death, such payment shall be made to the employee’s spouse or beneficiary.

Custodians that were hired after July 1 will receive pro-rated benefits on his/her date of hire. In
the transition year 2016-2017, all employees in the bargaining unit will be able to carry over
unused vacation time until July 2018. For school year 2017-2018, custodial maintenance
employees will receive their benefits beginning July 1%.


                                         ARTICLE XI

                                           HOLIDAYS
The following days shall be considered to be paid holidays for full time employees.

New Year’s Day                                        Washington’s Birthday
Patriot’s Day                                         Memorial Day
Independence Day                                      Labor Day
Columbus Day                                          Veteran’s Day
Martin Luther King Day                                Thanksgiving Day
Friday after Thanksgiving                             Full day before Christmas
Christmas Day                                         Full Day before New Year’s Day
Good Friday if no school all day                      Yom Kippur one-half (1/2) day
One-half (1/2) day Good Friday if school is in session for one-half (1/2) day

                                                 12
Except if any of the above holidays should occur when school must, for any reason, be in
session.

Should any holiday fall on an employee’s normal day off, the nearest scheduled working day will
be considered to be the holiday, except that should the school be in session on the nearest
scheduled working day, the day owed will be added to the vacation period. Holiday pay shall be
eight (8) hours pay at straight time rate. If a holiday occurs within an employee’s vacation
period, he shall receive an additional day’s vacation with pay.

Any employee required to work on a holiday shall receive, in addition to the regular holiday, an
amount equal to one and one-half times his regular rate of pay for all hours worked, but in no
case shall this be less than the amount equal to two (2) hours work at double time.

                                         ARTICLE XIII

                                         SICK LEAVE
Employees shall be granted fifteen (15) days of sick leave per year. Said accumulation shall be
allowed to accumulate from year to year to an unlimited number of days. By the middle of
August each year, all employees will receive written notice of all accumulated sick leave as of
the proceeding July 1".
Sick leave shall be granted for sickness or injury and for absence because of quarantine in the
family. In case of absence due to industrial accident, the employer agrees that the employee may
draw on his accumulated sick leave in an amount to make up the difference between his regular
weekly wage and his weekly payment under Worker’s Compensation and this may continue
during the said period of unemployment until his accumulated sick leave has been exhausted.
After five (5) days absence, a doctor’s certificate will be required (due to sickness or accident).

An employee is responsible for the appropriate use of sick leave. Sick leave abuse occurs when
an employee uses sick leave for the unauthorized purposes or misrepresents the actual reason for
charging an absence to sick leave. Abuse may occur when an employee establishes a pattern of
sick leave usage over a period of time such as the day before or after a holiday, on Mondays and
Fridays, after pay days, and any specific day, half day, or continued pattern of maintaining zero
to near zero balances. In an employee is suspected of sick leave abuse, he/she will be notified by
the immediate supervisor. If the pattern continues, progressive discipline will be implemented in
the following way:

    1 offense          Written warning
   2"4 offense         One (1) day suspension

   3" offense         Three (3) day suspension

   4" offense         Suspension with intention to dismiss

Absences related to an approved FMLA leave are not deemed as occurrences for the purposes of
this section, and are not subject to discipline.

                                                 13
Employees shall be paid a bonus equal to five (5) days for perfect attendance during the Fiscal
year’s 52 week period. Any member of the Union currently with 40 weeks will have the
opportunity to fulfill their 52 weeks. Employees shall be paid a bonus equal to two (2) days’ pay
for perfect attendance during the fiscal year’s consecutive 26 week work period between July 1
and December 31 or January 1 and June 1. The total bonus accumulated over a 52 week period
shall not exceed five (5) days per diem. Vacation and personal days shall not be taken into
consideration in determination of the 26 week and 52 week consecutive time periods. Days paid
shall be based on the per diem rate using the Salary Schedule in Appendix A included herein.

                                        ARTICLE XIV

                                          JURY DUTY
The employer agrees to make up the difference in an employee’s wages between a normal
week’s wages and compensation received for jury duty.

                                        ARTICLE XV

                                  CLOTHING ALLOWANCE
Effective July 1, 2014, employees will be reimbursed up to $300.00 per custodian and
maintenance employee. Receipts will be required before reimbursement can be paid. Approved
items to be reimbursed include work boots, sneakers, pants and shorts only.

The employer shall provide each employee up to six (6) standard shirts (long sleeve and short
sleeve, depending on each employee’s preference) as determined by the employer. The shirts
shall be required as standard work attire for all custodians and maintenance staff. Further, the
shirts are to be used only on the job in Georgetown. The cost of maintaining the shirts in proper
working condition (dry cleaning, laundering, etc.) shall be the responsibility of the employee.
The Employer shall replace such shirts on an as needed basis. The employer will provide Winter
gear upon hiring for employee use. This includes winter jacket, snow boots, hat and gloves. All
items must remain on site, and will be replaced as needed.

                                       ARTICLE XVI

                                 BEREAVEMENT            LEAVE
In the event of death in the immediate family of an employee, he will be granted leave in the
amount of up to three (3) working days, and such leave will not be charged to sick leave or
vacation leave. Additional time may be granted at the discretion of the Superintendent of
Schools.

Immediate family shall mean spouse, children, brother, sister, mother, father, mother-in-law,
father-in-law, grandchild, employee’s grandparents or a relative living in the employee’s
immediate household.




                                                14
One (1) day of funeral leave will be granted for the employee to attend the funeral of an aunt,
uncle, brother-in-law, sister-in-law or first (1) cousin.

                                         ARTICLE XVII

                                        PERSONAL LEAVE
Employee shall be granted three (3) days off per year for which he will be paid at his normal rate
to conduct imperative personal business or legal obligations, which could only be conducted
during regular working hours. Said leave shall not be deducted from any other paid leaves.
Except for emergencies, personal leave shall be requested well in advance of the day desired off.
Seventy-two hour notice must be provided and the approval of the Superintendent of Schools or
his designee is required.



                                        ARTICLE XVHUI

                                 SAFETY COMMITTEE CODE
A safety committee composed of two (2) representatives of the Union and two (2) employer
personnel will be formed to review safety practices. It may draw up a Safety Code, which both
parties to this Agreement agree to enforce, upon acceptance of the School Committee.

                                         ARTICLE XIX

                        CLASSIFICATION PLAN AND PAY RATES
In this Agreement and made part of it as Appendix A shall be established a Classification and
Pay Plan. It shall list all positions covered by this Agreement by title along with wages for each
position.

                                          ARTICLE XX

                               MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS
Should any provision of this Agreement be found to be in violation of any Federal or State law
by a Court of competent jurisdiction, all other provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full
force and effect for the duration of this Agreement, and any benefit, privilege or working
condition existing prior to this Agreement not specifically covered by this Agreement shall
remain in full force and effect. School Committee rules and regulations shall be inherent in the
Agreement by reference.

The parties to this Agreement agree that they shall not discriminate against any person because
of race, creed, color, sex or age and that such persons shall receive the full protection of this
Agreement.

The Employer, by Union notification to the Superintendent of Schools, agrees to permit
representatives of the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees, AFL-

                                                  15
 CIO, to enter the premises at any time for individual discussion of working conditions with
 employees, provided they do not interfere with the performance of duties assigned to the
 employees.

No one outside the bargaining unit shall perform work normally done by those employees within
the bargaining unit. This would not apply to part-time or temporary employees within the
bargaining unit. This would not apply to part-time or temporary help or those employed under
emergency of unusual circumstances.

There will be a Bulletin Board in each school at a place mutually agreed upon by custodians and
administration.

Each employee in the bargaining unit shall be allowed two (2) fifteen (15) minute breaks during
their shift.

Any permanent part-time employee, who becomes permanent after having completed one (1)
year of service as a permanent part-timer, said service shall count for the calculation of
vacations.

Employees will be available for training which may occur outside of normal work hours. The
employer will make every reasonable effort to give two weeks’ notice for said training.
Each year, and at the expense of the employer, the district will provide each employee with one
day of professional development which the date and subject matter of the course, seminar or
workshop are subject to the approval of the employer. This professional development day is an
additional day and is not included towards that employee’s vacation, sick or personal leave time.
Additional training requested by the employee will be considered provided that it is related to
their job description and approved by the employer.

Each employee shall be subject to a performance evaluation on an annual basis. The evaluation
is to be performed in accordance with the negotiated performance instrument. The union and
administration will develop written job descriptions upon which the evaluation instrument will
be based.

                                        ARTICLE XXI

                                  HEALTH AND          WELFARE
In the event the Great and General Court sees fit to increase the percentage payable by Cities and
Towns toward Blue Cross and Blue Shield Insurance and it is adopted by the Town of
Georgetown, the Committee will pay the said percentage adopted by the Town of Georgetown
permitted by Law effective the date and year designated by Town Meeting.

                                       ARTICLE XXII

                                   NO STRIKE PROVISION




                                                16
 Custodians agree that for the life of this Agreement, that they will not individually or collectively
 engage in, induce, or encourage any strike, work stoppage, slowdown or withholding their
 services.

                                          ARTICLE XXIII

                                     SICK    LEAVE      BUY    BACK
Any custodian about to retire under the provisions and guidelines of the Essex County
Retirement Board and covered by this agreement may obtain an increase in compensation due on
the custodian’s last paycheck by following the established procedure.

Eligible custodians who desire to participate in this program will notify the Superintendent of
Schools at least twelve (12) months prior to retirement date.

The final pay check of the custodian concerned will reflect a payment of $20.00 per day for each
sick leave day to which the retiree is entitled computed by subtracting the number of days absent
during the last twelve (12) months of employment from the total accumulated sick leave at the
time of retirement. Any employee not giving a twelve (12) month notice as defined above who
because of special circumstances, does give ninety (90) days’ notice of their intent under the
provisions listed above may receive sick leave buy back compensation as determined herein. If
the Committee because of the ninety-day notice is unable to fund the provisions of the sick leave
buy back that fiscal year, said Committee shall fund it the following fiscal year and it shall be
paid to the employee as soon as possible in the new fiscal year. Sick leave shall be capped at one
hundred fifty (150) days.

                                          ARTICLE XXIV

                                          AGENCY FEE
The Committee agrees to require as a condition of employment that all custodians except those
custodians certified as member to the Committee by the Union pay biweekly or by dues
deduction to the Union as of the thirtieth (30th) day subsequent to the effective date of this
agreement, or the thirtieth (30) day subsequent to the execution of this agreement, whichever is
later, an agency service fee which shall be commensurate with the cost of collective bargaining
and contract administration as determined solely by the Union and which amount shall be
certified annually to the Committee by the Union.

                                          ARTICLE XXV

                                  REDUCTION
                                          IN FORCE
If the Georgetown School Committee decides to reduce the number of positions in the bargaining
unit, each of the classifications listed below shall be considered a separate seniority unit:

                       a)   Custodians
                       b)   Maintenance

      Within each classification, employees shall be laid off in inverse order of seniority in their
     particular school. Employees targeted for layoff shall have the right to bump the least senior
     employee within their classification first if they are senior to, and qualified to perform the work
     at the prevailing rate of pay for such work. The affected employee shall be allowed to bump
     down to a lower classification bumping the least senior employee if the bumping employee is
     senior to that employee and qualified to perform the work at the prevailing rate of pay for such
     work. Any employee affected by the above procedure may elect to take the layoff instead of
     bumping.

     Employees laid off shall be kept on a recall list for one year and a day. [t shall be the
     responsibility of the laid off employce to keep the Superintendent’s Office informed of the
     employees current address. The Superintendent or a designee will send, by registered mail,
     return receipt requested, notice of any vacancy to which the laid off employee has recall rights.
     If the employee refuses the recall offer or fails to respond within ten days of either receipt of the
     notice or first attempt to deliver the notice, whichever is first, all recall rights will terminate.

    If there is an increase in any classification in the bargaining unit after a layoff, employees shall
    be recalled in inverse order of their layoff to position they are qualified to perform at the
    prevailing tate of pay for such work.
